truth.papers.A39.title: Formation of precipitates and their effects on the mechanical properties in a Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel
truth.papers.A39.paper_scope: This study investigates the effects of Ti and V additions on κ-carbide precipitation behavior and mechanical properties in Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel, proposing a strategy using (Ti,V)C carbides as carbon sinks to control carbon distribution and optimize strength and work-hardening.

prediction.papers.A39.title: Formation of precipitates and their effects on the mechanical properties in a Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel
prediction.papers.A39.paper_scope: Investigating the effects of Ti and V microalloying on precipitation behavior and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steels, specifically through the formation of (Ti,V)C carbides to control heterogeneous κ-carbide precipitation and optimize the balance between strength and work-hardening capacity.

truth.alloys.alloy_m2.alloys_notes: The higher carbon content in M2 (0.85 wt%) compared to M1 (0.61 wt%) is mainly to compensate for carbon consumed by (Ti,V)C formation.

truth.processes.proc_m1_ss.description: Hot rolling at 1000 °C from 20 to 2 mm, water quenching; solution treatment at 1000 °C for 30 min, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_m1_aged30.description: Hot rolling + solution treatment (as above) + aging at 550 °C for 30 h, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_m2_ss.description: Hot rolling at 1000 °C from 20 to 2 mm, water quenching; solution treatment at 1000 °C for 30 min, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_m2_aged10.description: Hot rolling + solution treatment (as above) + aging at 550 °C for 10 h, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_m2_aged30.description: Hot rolling + solution treatment (as above) + aging at 550 °C for 30 h, water quenching.

prediction.processes.proc_m1_ss.description: Vacuum arc melted, hot rolled from 20 mm to 2 mm at 1000°C and water quenched, then solution treated at 1000°C for 30 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_m1_aged30.description: Vacuum arc melted, hot rolled from 20 mm to 2 mm at 1000°C and water quenched, solution treated at 1000°C for 30 min and water quenched, followed by aging at 550°C for 30 h and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_m2_ss.description: Vacuum arc melted, hot rolled from 20 mm to 2 mm at 1000°C and water quenched, then solution treated at 1000°C for 30 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_m2_aged10.description: Vacuum arc melted, hot rolled from 20 mm to 2 mm at 1000°C and water quenched, solution treated at 1000°C for 30 min and water quenched, followed by aging at 550°C for 10 h and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_m2_aged30.description: Vacuum arc melted, hot rolled from 20 mm to 2 mm at 1000°C and water quenched, solution treated at 1000°C for 30 min and water quenched, followed by aging at 550°C for 30 h and water quenched.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_m1_30.processing: Thickness reduced from 20 mm to 2 mm.

truth.structures.sample_m1_ss.overall_structur: Single-phase austenitic matrix with equiaxed grains, no precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_m1_30.overall_structur: Austenitic matrix with uniformly distributed κ-carbides.
truth.structures.sample_m1_30.microstructure_l: uniformly distributed in austenite matrix, number density ~7×10^22 m−3
truth.structures.sample_m2_ss.overall_structur: Austenitic matrix with (Ti,V)C carbides precipitated at high temperature.
truth.structures.sample_m2_ss.microstructure_l: equiaxed grains, bimodal grain size distribution
bimodal, some small grains embedded around larger ones
non-shearable (Orowan bypass), presumably incoherent or semi-coherent
truth.structures.sample_m2_10.overall_structur: Austenitic matrix with (Ti,V)C carbides and κ-carbides preferentially precipitated at edges of (Ti,V)C.
truth.structures.sample_m2_10.microstructure_l: heterogeneously precipitated at edges of (Ti,V)C carbides, almost no κ in matrix
truth.structures.sample_m2_30.overall_structur: Austenitic matrix with (Ti,V)C carbides and κ-carbides heterogeneously distributed at (Ti,V)C edges.
truth.structures.sample_m2_30.microstructure_l: equiaxed grains, more unimodal after further growth
heterogeneous, predominantly at edges of (Ti,V)C carbides, volume fraction 3.2 ± 2.8%

prediction.structures.sample_m1_ss.overall_structur: Single-phase austenite with equiaxed grains, no precipitates
prediction.structures.sample_m1_30.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with uniformly distributed intragranular κ-carbides
prediction.structures.sample_m2_10.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with (Ti,V)C carbides and heterogeneous κ-carbides precipitated at (Ti,V)C edges
prediction.structures.sample_m2_10.microstructure_l: heterogeneous precipitation at edges of (Ti,V)C carbides
prediction.structures.sample_m2_30.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with (Ti,V)C carbides and heterogeneous κ-carbides at (Ti,V)C edges
prediction.structures.sample_m2_30.microstructure_l: heterogeneous precipitation at edges of (Ti,V)C carbides

truth.interfaces.sample_m1_30.defect_interacti: Dislocations shear κ-carbides, destroying local order and causing glide plane softening.
truth.interfaces.sample_m1_30.phase_evolution: Homogeneous precipitation of κ-carbides through spinodal decomposition during aging.
truth.interfaces.sample_m2_30.defect_interacti: κ-carbides can be sheared, but heterogeneous distribution mitigates glide plane softening.
Dislocations bow around non-shearable (Ti,V)C carbides, creating high dislocation density regions and increasing flow stress.
truth.interfaces.sample_m2_30.phase_evolution: (Ti,V)C precipitates at high temperature act as carbon sinks; κ-carbides nucleate heterogeneously at carbon-enriched edges during aging.

prediction.interfaces.sample_m2_30.phase_evolution: κ-carbides preferentially precipitate at the edge of (Ti,V)C carbides during aging, utilizing surrounding carbon-rich regions as heterogeneous nucleation sites.

truth.properties.sample_m1_ss.mechanical.tensi: Engineering stress-strain curve presented in Fig. 7a.
truth.properties.sample_m1_30.mechanical.tensi: Engineering stress-strain curve presented in Fig. 7a; work-hardening rate significantly reduced after aging.
truth.properties.sample_m2_ss.mechanical.tensi: Engineering stress-strain curve presented in Fig. 7a.
truth.properties.sample_m2_30.mechanical.tensi: Engineering stress-strain curve presented in Fig. 7a; work-hardening rate maintained comparable to solution-treated state.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_m1_30.ke: Yield strength 860 MPa, UTS 1004 MPa, elongation 40%, reduced work-hardening rate.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_m2_30.ke: Yield strength 884 MPa, UTS 1096 MPa, work-hardening rate maintained.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_m2_10.ke: (Ti,V)C carbides present; κ-carbides preferentially precipitate at edges of (Ti,V)C, almost no κ in matrix.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m1_ss.pu: Mechanical properties evaluation at room temperature
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m1_ss.ke: Yield strength 430 MPa; tested at strain rate 10^-3 s^-1 with 12.5 mm extensometer along rolling direction.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m1_30.pu: Dislocation configuration and deformation mode after tensile testing
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m1_30.ke: Planar slip of dislocation observed in the uniformly deformed region.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_ss.pu: Mechanical properties evaluation at room temperature
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_ss.ke: Yield strength ~630 MPa (narrative) / 660 MPa (Fig. 10), approximately 200 MPa higher than M1-SS; tested at strain rate 10^-3 s^-1.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_30.pu: Dislocation configuration and deformation mode after tensile testing
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_30.ke: Planar slip with significantly narrower slip-band spacing than M1-30; intense plastic deformation and dislocation accumulation around (Ti,V)C carbides.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_10.te: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) with bright-field (BF), dark-field (DF), and SAED
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_10.pu: Precipitate morphology and distribution after 10 h aging
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m2_10.ke: κ-carbides and (Ti,V)C carbides coexist; κ-carbides preferentially precipitate at the edges of (Ti,V)C carbides with almost no matrix precipitation.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Olson-Cohen thermodynamic model for stacking fault energy
Prediction of SFE for M1 steel composition
ρ = 2.8e-5 mol/m², ΔG^{γ→ε} from thermodynamic data, σ^{γ/ε} = 10 mJ/m², ΔG_ex ≈ 1-2 mJ/m²; calculated SFE ≈ 70 mJ/m².
SFE value indicates deformation mode is dislocation slip.
truth.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Olson-Cohen thermodynamic model for stacking fault energy
Prediction of SFE for M2 steel composition
ρ = 2.9e-5 mol/m², ΔG^{γ→ε} from thermodynamic data, σ^{γ/ε} = 10 mJ/m², ΔG_ex ≈ 1-2 mJ/m²; calculated SFE ≈ 78 mJ/m².
Ti and V have little influence on SFE; deformation mode remains dislocation slip.
truth.computational_details.sample_m1_ss.analy: Strain hardening exponent and stages analysis for M1-SS
Equation: ln(dσ/dε) = (n-1) ln ε + ln(n k). Work hardening divided into three stages.
truth.computational_details.sample_m1_30.analy: Strain hardening exponent and stages analysis for M1-30
After aging, work hardening divided into two stages; rapid decrease in first stage due to dislocation slip plane softening.
truth.computational_details.sample_m2_ss.analy: Work hardening index curves almost coincide before and after aging, indicating maintained work-hardening ability.
truth.computational_details.sample_m2_30.analy: Work hardening index remains high, attributed to heterogeneous κ-carbide distribution and (Ti,V)C Orowan bypass.
truth.computational_details.idx_6.analytical_m: Yield strength superposition and precipitation strengthening models
Quantification of strengthening contributions in M1-30 and M2-30

σ_y = σ_0 + σ_G + σ_D + σ_P. Hall-Petch: σ_G = k_y D^{-1/2}, k_y=683 MPa·μm^{1/2}. κ-carbides shearing by coherency, modulus mismatch, order strengthening; (Ti,V)C Orowan bypass. Parameters: M=3.06, α=2.6, ε_c=0.6, G=70.7 GPa, b=0.26 nm, ΔG=23.7 GPa, m=0.85, γ_apb from literature.
Quantitative results shown in Fig. 10: M1-SS σ_0=350, σ_G=80; M1-30 σ_0=350, σ_G=90, σ_S=430; M2-SS σ_0=410, σ_G=100, σ_Or=120; M2-30 σ_0=410, σ_G=100, σ_Or=120, σ_S=220 MPa.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Olson-Cohen thermodynamic model for stacking fault energy (SFE) calculation
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated SFE values (~70 mJ/m^2 for M1 and ~78 mJ/m^2 for M2) consistent with observed dislocation slip deformation mode by TEM.

Precipitation strengthening σ_S = σ_cs + σ_ms + σ_os
M = 3.06, α = 2.6, ε_c = 0.6, G = 70.7 GPa, b = 0.26 nm, ΔG = 23.7 GPa, m = 0.85; r and f from experimental measurement
Includes coherency strengthening, modulus-mismatch strengthening, and order strengthening; chemical and stacking-fault strengthening neglected.
prediction.computational_details.sample_m2_30.valid: Calculated contributions compared with measured yield strength in Fig. 10.
prediction.computational_details.sample_m2_30.analy: k_y = 683 MPa·μm^1/2, D = average grain size
G = 70.7 GPa, b = 0.26 nm, d = diameter of (Ti,V)C, f = volume fraction
Precipitation strengthening σ_S = σ_cs + σ_ms + σ_os
M = 3.06, α = 2.6, ε_c = 0.6, G = 70.7 GPa, b = 0.26 nm, ΔG = 23.7 GPa, m = 0.85; r and f from experimental measurement
Applied to κ-carbides precipitated at (Ti,V)C edges; coherency, modulus-mismatch, and order strengthening included.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 1 shows optical micrographs of M1 and M2 specimens; all exhibit equiaxed grain morphology. Grain sizes: M1-SS 42.4±7.8 μm, M1-30 45.3±9.3 μm, M2-SS 32.3±10.4 μm, M2-30 35.8±12.7 μm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fig. 2: EBSD IPF maps indicate no significant texture in M2-SS and M2-30; grain size distribution evolves from bimodal to unimodal after aging.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 3: TEM BF images show no precipitates in M1-SS, while M2-SS contains (Ti,V)C carbides with size 35.8±20.1 nm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 4: TEM micrographs of precipitates; κ-carbides in M1-30 are uniformly distributed with number density ~7×10^22 m−3; in M2-10 and M2-30, κ preferentially precipitates at (Ti,V)C edges.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fig. 5: TEM-EDS mapping of M2-30 shows V enrichment in (Ti,V)C; no Mn or Al enrichment in κ-carbides, suggesting ordering mechanism rather than compositional partitioning.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fig. 6: HRTEM shows intergrowth of κ-carbide and (Ti,V)C with orientation relationship (002)(Ti,V)C || (001)κ; κ-carbides appear linearly aligned along (Ti,V)C edges.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 7: Tensile curves; M1-SS YS ~430 MPa, M1-30 YS 860 MPa, elongation 40%, work-hardening rate drops; M2-SS YS ~660 MPa, M2-30 YS 884 MPa, UTS 1096 MPa, work-hardening rate maintained.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fig. 8: EPMA of M2-SS shows carbon enrichment at center of (Ti,V)C and gradient decrease toward edges, indicating carbon sink effect.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fig. 9: Schematic of microstructural evolution in M2 during aging: pre-existing (Ti,V)C with carbon-enriched zones, κ-carbides precipitate at edges.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fig. 10: Quantified strengthening contributions: M1-30 primarily κ-carbide shearing (~430 MPa); M2-30 receives ~220 MPa from κ shearing and ~120 MPa from (Ti,V)C Orowan bypass.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Fig. 11: Crussard-Jaoul plots and work hardening index analysis; M1-SS three stages, M1-30 only two stages with rapid drop; M2-SS and M2-30 curves almost coincide.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Fig. 12: Post-tensile TEM of M1-30 and M2-30; both show planar slip, but M2-30 exhibits finer slip band spacing and more intense plastic deformation, with (Ti,V)C hindering dislocations.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Deformation mode for both steels is planar dislocation slip, consistent with high SFE (~70-78 mJ/m²).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Addition of Ti and V refines grain size from 42.4±7.8 μm to 32.3±10.4 μm in solution-treated state.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: κ-carbide precipitation mechanism: spinodal decomposition in carbon-supersaturated matrix; uniform when carbon is supersaturated, heterogeneous when carbon content is reduced by (Ti,V)C sinks.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: Glide plane softening effect: shearable κ-carbides reduce friction stress for subsequent dislocations, lowering work-hardening rate; heterogeneous dispersion mitigates this effect.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_16.value: Proposed novel microalloying strategy: use strong carbide-forming elements (Ti, V) to form high-temperature (Ti,V)C as carbon sinks, altering κ-carbide distribution and preserving work-hardening capacity while increasing strength.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 10 tabulates strengthening mechanism contributions to yield strength: M1-SS measured 430 MPa (σ0=350, σG=80), M1-30 measured 860 MPa (σ0=350, σG=90, σS=430), M2-SS measured 660 MPa (σ0=410, σG=100, σOr=120), M2-30 measured 880 MPa (σ0=410, σG=100, σOr=120, σS=220). These tabulated values show minor discrepancies with narrative statements (M2-SS described as ~200 MPa higher than M1-SS; M2-30 stated as 884 MPa).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fig. 7 presents engineering stress-strain and true stress-strain curves with strain hardening rate curves for M1-SS, M1-30, M2-SS, and M2-30; specific UTS and elongation values for M1-SS and M2-SS are not explicitly stated in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 11 presents Crussard-Jaoul work-hardening index plots (ln(dσ/dε) vs lnε) showing three stages for M1-SS and two stages for M1-30, whereas M2-SS and M2-30 curves nearly coincide, indicating that the uniform precipitation of κ-carbides reduces work-hardening capacity while Ti/V modification preserves it.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: In M1-30, TEM DF imaging revealed a number density of uniformly distributed κ-carbides of approximately 7×10^22 m^-3.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Calculated stacking fault energies are approximately 70 mJ/m^2 for M1 and 78 mJ/m^2 for M2 based on the Olson-Cohen model, indicating dislocation slip as the predominant deformation mode.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The tensile strength increased from 1004 MPa (M1-30) to 1096 MPa (M2-30), but the text does not express this improvement as a percentage.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 9 schematically illustrates the microstructural evolution in M2 steel: preexisting (Ti,V)C carbides are surrounded by carbon-enriched zones, and upon aging at 550°C κ-carbides preferentially precipitate along the edges of (Ti,V)C carbides.
